There are very few books I deem as "must reads," but this is one of them—a profoundly moving story about how, against all odds, people try to be good to one another despite the devastation and evil around them. This book truly took my breath away with its poetic sentence structure combined with its shorter chapters making it a difficult book to put down once started. Anthony Doerr's attention to detail paints such vivid imagery that I could clearly see Marie-Laure’s Parisian streets, Werner’s brutal academy life and ultimately the bombing of Saint Malo in my mind's eye while reading every single page leading up until its hauntingly beautiful climax which will stick with me forevermore. All The Light We Cannot See is a masterful blend of historical fiction that captures both darkness and light during WWII times showcasing what it means to survive amidst impossible circumstances through resilience, hope, love…and ultimately redemption even if found later down

texts.full_text: Diggle (2013), Møller and Waagepeterse(2004). In recent years, the availability of large spatio-temporal datasets has increased interest in modeling point processes with complex dependence structures. One such area where this is particularly relevant is urban mobility studies involving bike-sharing systems. Bike sharing programs have become a popular mode of transportation and are widely used for short trips within cities around the world (Shaheen et al., 2019). These services generate vast amounts of spatio-temporal data, which can be analyzed to understand usage patterns. However, modeling such processes is challenging due to their inherent complexity: bike rentals exhibit both spatial clustering and temporal dynamics that are often interrelated. To add

texts.full_text: a claim to exemptions), then that civil proceeding is one `arising under title 11'." 1 Collier on Bankruptcy (15th Edition) ¶ 3.01, p. 3-40. There are few cases in existence to aid this Court's interpretation of 28 U.S.C. *193 § 1471(b). The cases which do exist are illustrative of the application of Section 1471(b), but they do not define the extent of the jurisdictional grant contained in this statute. In In Re: Jewel Terrace Corporation, 3 B.R. 36, 1 C.B.C.2d 581 (Bkrtcy.Ct.E.D.N.Y. 1980) the court found that it had jurisdiction to hear the debtor's claim for rent monies collected by tenant's organization. However, the court declined to take jurisdiction for the reason that a state court proceeding had been initiated prior to the filing of the bankruptcy petition. An action brought by the trustee in bankruptcy against a creditor of the debtor's which involved issues under the Truth in Lending Act was heard by the bankruptcy court in In Re: Claypool, 2 C.B.C.2d 64 (M.D.Fla. 1980). The debtors in In Re: Griffith, 6 B.R. 750, 2 C.B.C.2d 387 (Bkrtcy.Ct.D.N. M.1980) brought an action to rescind a contract involving the only significant asset of the debtor's. It was held that the court had jurisdiction to hear this dispute and ancillary jurisdiction to hear the cross-claims for contribution and indemnity of the defendants. In In Re: Trim-Lean Meat Products, Inc., 4 B.R. 243, 2 C.B.C.2d 117 (Bkrtcy.Ct. D.Del.1980) the court heard a case on the appropriation of a corporate opportunity by officers and directors of the debtor corporation. These cases illustrate the broad range of matters over which the bankruptcy courts now have jurisdiction. However it must be noted that in each of the above-cited cases either the debtor, an asset of the debtor's estate or a claim of the debtor's was involved in the matter heard. Life He was born in Mbeni, Grande Comore and issued family privileges, he was the benefiaciary of Djumbé Fumu, descendant of Sultan Msa Fumu. He studied in Madagascar as a child then went to France to continue his education and obtain an engineering degree in public works. Returning to Grande Comore, Said Mohamed Cheikh made him responsible for the public works. In September 1975 he was appointed Minister of Public Works, Transport and Telecommunications under President Ahmed Abdallah Abderemane . On 30 December 1977, he became Prime Minister and was elected President of the National Assembly on 22 May

texts.full_text: ul economic and altruistic roots, and may be socially beneficial in problematizing and solving the rampant excess of consumer culture, but it can also be understood as a fig leaf of sorts. When I was growing up, old people who saved string, tin foil, and paper bags seemed like damaged goods, crazy holdovers from the anomalous scarcity of the great depression. Today, it's becoming increasing hip and cool to do exactly these things -- in other words, we've developed a cultural mechanism couching the degradations of poverty in both the attractiveness of hipster culture and the admirability of altruism. Today's string-savers aren't a bunch of crazy old kooks; they're cutting-edge, avant-garde paragons of youth culture, and staunch defenders of our planet's future.Please, somebody tell me I'm wrong... texts.full_text: electrical circuits having electronic circuit boards. In many of these electronic devices the circuit boards comprise a dielectric substrate having a conductor path formed thereon. With respect to many of these prior art circuit boards the dielectric substrate may comprise alumina (Al.sub.2 O.sub.3) or beryllium oxide (Beo) and the conductor path may be formed on the substrate utilizing such metals titanium (Ti), molybdenum (Mo) and manganese (Mn). Such conductor paths are generally formed upon the dielectric substrates by first producing a conductor paste and then applying the paste tosaid circuit board. In certain situations it is desirable that prior art electronic systems operate at altitudes of about 250.degree. C., for instance, computer or TV sets which might be located in an automobile where such low temperature operation may happen when exposed directly (i.e.; no air flow) from a cold engine compartment during the winter hours. It could therefore not prove useful if circuit boards can withstand operating conditions at these lower altitudes. A main advantage of utilizing aluminum as dielectric substrate is that it tends to become very fragile and has weak structural stability, even when exposEd or exposed for long periods (i.e.; 1000 days) above about the aforementioned temperature. In order overcome this issue prior art circuit boards are often built with aluminum oxide which does not have these problems. While such electronic systems utilizing low dielectric constant substrates can operate at elevated altitudes, in certain cases it is generally desirable that said substrate materials be able to withstand and sustain a lower voltage potential (i.e.; 2000 volts) for extended periods of time. The aforementioned aluminum alloy does not meet this criterion because the minimum sustained electric field strength available with previous art circuit boards utilizing such dielectric substrates has been about 10000 v/mil. The concern associated when designing digital systems using low voltage potential is that it should be desirable to have an insulating material which can resist a lower electrical stress. It should therefore prove beneficial if the various substrate components were able (i.e.; at elevated altitudes)

texts.full_text: success, we have devised a strategy that identifies the best stocks that best-performing hedge funds are collectively bullish on. This approach has returned over 74% when our strategy was launched in May 2014, outperforming the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) by over 20 percentage points. The stock picks that are part of our strategy are shared in our premium quarterly newsletters, which you can access now free of charge for up to 14 days. Before we get to its picks, a couple of words about the fund and its manager. Mr. Silverman changed the fund’s name into Opaleye from Risk Reward Capital and registered the fund with the SEC in 2013. In 2011, Mr. Silverman and RRC were involved in a lawsuit alleging insider trading. Mr. Silverman was accused of using information provided by doctors working as consultants for Guidepoint Global LLC. Massachusetts Secretary of the Commonwealth William Galvin said in the complaint that access to Guidepoint helped RRC Bio Fund generate strong returns of 55% in 2009 and 52% in 2010, while when Mr. Silverman began trading in 2007, it lost 17%. After he began paying Guidepoint for access to doctors, Silverman made bets on Ariad Pharmaceuticals and Questcor, which proved very profitable. However, Mr. Silverman denied the accusations and said that he outperformed his peers during the market drop of 2007. He also said that he invested in Questcor before getting information from consultants and continued to hold the stock. The investigation into Mr. Silverman continued well into 2013, when his fund’s assets reached $100 million and he had to register it with the Securities and Exchange Commission. In 2013, Silverman filed a complaint against Galvin, saying that the Secretary of the Commonwealth was meddling in its transition to federal registration, after his office reversed its decision to grant Opaleye’s request to withdraw its state registration. In 2014, Silverman and the office of the Secretary of the Commonwealth entered into a Consent Order and agreed to settle the matter, with Opaleye paying a $50,000 fine,

texts.full_text: where they encountered both isolated villages preserving ancient customs and bustling towns transformed by rapid globalization. Wearing traditional woolen (woolen socks) borrowed from local Shekhpa tribespeople, the midshipmen navigated snow-capped passes like the Baralacha La, pausing to document how GPS-enabled trekking apps coexisted with generations-old oral navigation techniques passed down by villagers. In the hamlet of Tabo, they participated in a 1,000-year-old Tibetan Buddhist Losar New Year celebration, noticing how solar-powered sound systems replaced traditional drummers for event coordination, while monks in handwoven karuna robes still conducted morning meditation rituals. At Leh’s Shanti Stupa, a massive Buddhist peace pagoda built in 1991, the group observed the juxtaposition of tourists snapping drone footage and elderly locals performing circumambulations with handcrafted mala prayer beads, underscoring the friction between heritage and contemporary technology. The most poignant reflection came during a homestay in the village of Zum, where the midshipmen lived with Bhotiya tribal families. As they learned the nuances of (dried yak cheese) making—a practice unchanged for centuries—their hosts marveled at the midshipmen’s instant translation apps and satellite phones. One midshipman noted, *“It struck me how our technologies flatten linguistic barriers, yet the Bhotiya’s use of mountain flora for healing remains encoded

texts.full_text: rom identifying mechanisms of disease to rapidly testing new drugs. However, animal models have certain drawbacks. Due in part to differences in anatomy, biochemistry, genetics, and physiology, the disease may manifest slightly differently in animals and humans, and the effects of pharmacological treatments may differ between human and animal cells. Some limitations of animal models potentially could be overcome by developing human cell models of aging and disease. Scientists have found that human adult fibroblasts (skin cells) and other cell types can be “reprogrammed” into induced pluripotent stem (iPS) cells, which have the capacity to differentiate into any other type of cell. iPS cells offer enormous potential for generating age-specific and disease-specific cells for analyses of aging and disease mechanisms in human cells. In the near term, human iPS cells could be used for disease modeling to provide insight into disease mechanisms and as platforms for drug screening and testing. Eventually, iPS cells may be used for cell and tissue replacement therapies. In 2011, NIH issued an FOA soliciting research applications on the development of human iPS cells and other reprogrammed cells for aging and Alzheimer’s disease modeling. Findings from these studies could energize the field by providing an inexpensive, renewable, and more precise model for discovery and testing of interventions. For more

The house was built in 1844 and burned down in 1932. Nettlehirst House and estate The 1856 OS map shows an 'L' shaped building at the site of the later 'castle' with a detached outbuilding nearby and a saw pit close to the house. A Nettlehirst Townhead is marked nearby, lying to the north-west of the house. Few trees are indicated and the mausoleum was not yet built. The IETF (Internet Engineering Task Force) and W3C (World Wide Web Consortium) are two distinct and important organizations, each with distinct goals and scopes, although the overarching aim, as you mentioned, is to improve the Internet and the Web. The IETF is the primary Internet standards organization, with the primary aim to develop and promote standards and protocols to ensure the smooth and secure operation and interoperability of the Internet. The standards and protocols IETF is responsible for include the Internet Protocol, the TCP/IP protocols, and the protocols for the Web, and the Internet of Things. IETF standards, known as RFCs, are detailed, and the RFCs are the result of the IETF's collaborative and open standards development. texts.full_text: Seine in Paris. Picture: Getty Images These days you might visit the Moulin Rouge in Paris for its world-famous dancers or take a leisurely cruise along the River Seine. Try a city tour with a local expert, or smile back enigmatically at the Mona Lisa in the Louvre. More Europe inspiration: On To Italy Next stop would be Switzerland – Geneva or Lausanne – before heading into northern Italy. These days you can see the highlights of Geneva, ‘the city of gardens’, such as the Jet D’Eau, Floral Clock and Reformation Monument. The Uffizi is a must in Florence. Picture: Getty Images Once in Italy, travellers of yore would spend several months in Florence, with the Uffizi figuring largely in their learning. Travellers today can still visit the Uffizi, and gaze upon such wonders as Botticelli’s Birth of Venus, da Vinci’s Annunciation, and Caravaggio’s Medusa, just as noblemen and women did 200 years before. Don’t forget to pop over to the Accademia for Michelangelo’s astonishing David. La Serenissima was another major stop on the Grand Tour – seen more as a place of decadence than learning. These days the visitor to Venice might cruise to St Mark’s Square, for a water’s eye view of the Bridge of Sighs and the Doge’s Palace. Watch glass-making on Giudecca Island or indulge in Venetian cuisine on the island of Burano. Art & Archaeology Rome was another cornerstone of the Grand Tour, a place to study the masterpieces of painting, sculpture and architecture, as well as archaeology. Do not miss the Vatican Museums – where your tour may gain you VIP access. Admire Michelangelo’s Sistine Chapel and enjoy an after-hours dinner at the Missionary Ethnological Museum. texts.full_text: years and education Haddad was born to Palestinian Christian (Greek Orthodox) parents in Safed, Palestine, in 1927. During the 1948 Arab–Israeli War his family's home was destroyed and his family fled to Lebanon. He studied medicine at the American University of Beirut, where he met fellow Palestinian refugee, George Habash, who was also a medical student. Together they helped found the Arab Nationalist Movement (ANM), a pan-Arabist and Arab socialist group aiming to create the State of Palestine and unite the Arab countries. After graduating,Haddad returned to Lebanon and worked as a physician. However, he was increasingly drawn to political activism and became involved with the Palestinian Liberation Organization (PLO) and its armed wing, the Popular Front for the Liberation of Palestine (PFLP). Haddad's experiences as a physician and his deep commitment to the Palestinian cause made him a key figure in the PFLP's armed struggle. In the early 1960s, Haddad organized his first hijacking, targeting a civilian airplane in order to demand the release of Palestinian prisoners and the establishment of a Palestinian state. This hijacking, along with others that followed, drew international attention to the PFLP's tactics and the plight of the Palestinian people. Haddad's actions were controversial, with some critics arguing that his tactics were too extreme and counterproductive. However, others saw

texts.full_text: if the player moves backwards then the firing rate is essentially slower (at least until they reach the edge of the screen) and vice versa if you move forwards. A: It is classical Doppler effect, which is quite normal thing. If you move backwards, then the distance between bullets is normal_distance_caused_by_firerate - ship_backward_distance_passed, instaead of just normal_distance_caused_by_firerate if ship stands still. If you increase the bullets speed, then this effect would be harder to notice, because the distance would be bigger by itself, compared to the ship speed. You could increase the firerate if ship's moving back, and decrease it when it's moving forward, but this isn't normal behavior, and it could look weird

texts.full_text: which number to go with? Keep reading for more factors to consider.Your family. The first thing to consider is that the budget includes everyone in your family - so if you have children, then their clothing also needs to come out of this budget. If you are single, then the budget is just for you.Your job. Your job is going to be another huge factor in deciding how much money to set aside for clothes. Some jobs, such as working in a law office or a fashion magazine, are going to requireclothing that is higher quality and of more variety, and therefore you should create a larger budget. A job with a uniform, however, should save you a considerable amount on clothing since you only need clothes for weekends and fun occasions.Your financial goals. This is another huge factor to consider. Are you saving money for a house? Are you paying off credit card debt? If you have significant financial goals or stressors, then you will want to reduce your clothing budget as much as possible to make room for these other (more important) categories.

texts.full_text: he last of my workshop cabinets. It’ a five drawer cabinet with extension slides to house my machine vice, grinder and metal working tools (perish the thought). This cabinet sees the last of the laminate MDF panels I bought on the cheap from ebay. All was going well untill I made the drawer boxes the same width as the inside measurement as the cabinet. I don’t know what I was thinking as I even measured it twice, but clearly didn't account for the thickness of the drawer sides or any necessary clearance. It’s a common mistake to forget about these but crucial details. Fortunately, I managed to shave down the sides and adjust the slides slightly to get everything working I’ve learned my lesson though—next time, I’ll remember to subtract at least 1/8 inch per side for clearance when planning drawer dimensions. Despite this hiccup, I’m really pleased with how it turned out. The laminate gives it a professional look and the full extension slides make accessing tools a breeze. texts.full_text: a lean burn internal combustion engine, and in particular, relates to a control of the temperature of a lean sensor used in such an engine for a detection of the air-fuel ratio therein. 2. Description of Related Art Known in a prior art is a lean burn combustion engine having an operating area, for example, a low load condition, wherein a lean combustible mixture is introduced into the engine to increase the fuel consumption efficiency. In the lean burn engine, a basic fuel injection amount, which is an amount of fuel needed for providing a theoretical air-fuel ratio at a combination of an engine speed and an engine load parameter such as the intake pressure, is first calculated and then, to obtain the lean air-fuel mixture, a lean correction factor having a value smaller than 1.0 is multiplied by the basic fuel injection amount. A lean correction factor map is provided and is constructed by values of the lean correction factor with respect to combinations of the engine speed and the intake pressure. When the engine enters a power area from the lean area, due to a depression of an accelerator pedal, aleaning rate change becomes very large or even infinite in some cases since it is not only determining how much fuel goes into each cylinder but also controlling what type and quantity. texts.full_text: the potential of methane reoccupation during the replacement of methane hydrate by CO(2). Molecular dynamics simulations and stabilization energy calculations are performed in this work in order to understand the stability of CH(4) hydrate, CO(2) hydrate, and CH(4)-CO(2) mixed hydrate. The model systems of fully occupied type SI CH(4) hydrate, CO(2) hydrate, and CH(4)-cO(2) mixed hydrate are prepared in a simulation box with periodic boundary conditions. The force field parameters for methane and carbon dioxide are taken from the literature, and the interactions between the guest molecules and the water framework are described using the Coulombic and Lennard-Jones potentials. The simulations are carried out at different temperatures and pressures to mimic the conditions under which methane hydrate and CO(2) hydrate are formed and to investigate the potential of methane reoccupation during the replacement process.
